tax: Placed at family rank, Terrae-Kin is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a bipedal fault-pressure stone body with persistent humanoid proportions and orbital glows, not a walker or roller. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a bipedal stone body with orbital glows and plinth-foot impressions.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a stone humanoid ranging 1.2–6 m with amber orbital hollows. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — orbital amber glow and plinth-impressions rather than round boulder-prints — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Terrae-Kin occupies fault zones, shallow seams, and convergent deep faults throughout the tiers. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where fault pressure shapes stone into a bipedal form and recur with the fault re-pressurises and the bipedal form re-assembles, sustaining themselves on fault pressure at depth.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: all Terrae-Kin are territorial, set rigid when resting, and sense approach through the floor; the nobilis form is additionally reported to signal intent. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: bipedal stone body, amber orbital glow, plinth-foot field sign, fault dependence, and vibration failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: mass strikes, stone incorporation, floor-sensing, and rigid disguise. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Terrae-Kin matters because accounts for most formally recorded Throat fatalities in fault zones. It lets survey reports sort humanoid stone hazards by size and stone type without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Terrae-Kin is the family-rank grouping of roughly humanoid fault-pressure stone elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a bipedal body of stone shaped by fault pressure into a recognisably humanoid form with orbital glows, and divide below into genus groups by fault depth and stone type, which determine the four body-plans of the Terrae genus. The Terrae-Kin family formalises the classic 'walking stone person' of grey-folk tradition as a distinct family within Lithic, separated from the Boulder-Kin by persistent humanoid form.
